Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-30-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff DAVID FENTON's Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Judgment.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff David Fenton's Motion for Entry of Judgment pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. A moving party must provide written notice of her motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, meaning all moving and supporting papers must be filed and served no fewer than 16 court days before the hearing. (See Code Civ. Proc., 1005, subd. (b).) A party serving by mail must add five calendar days to the notice period. Here, Plaintiff noticed his motion for hearing on June 30, 2023, but served his motion and supporting papers by mail on June 7, 2023, which was less than 16 court days plus 5 calendar days before the hearing-three court days short of the required notice period. Note that June 19, 2024, was a court holiday and, therefore, it was not a "court day."

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2021 Continued To Feb-09-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LATISHA TAYLOR'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT:

Defendant Latisha Taylor's demurrer to the sixth cause of action of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint (FAC) is overruled.

Plaintiff alleges that defendant fraudulently induced him to invest $60,000 in a technology company, Shoclef Corporation Limited, that was to be acquired by a third party, and that after the predicted sale failed to materialize, defendant falsely promised to remit a portion of his investment and to enter into a repayment plan, but never repaid the funds. In the sixth cause of action for violation of California Penal Code section 496, plaintiff alleges that defendant has unlawfully converted his $60,000 investment to her own use, and that he is entitled under section 496 to an award of treble damages, costs of suit, and reasonable attorney's fees. Defendant contends that damages under Penal Code 496 are unavailable based on a showing of fraudulent conduct in connection with a business arrangement. Based on current controlling authority, the Court disagrees. Section 496 applies to property "that has been obtained in any manner constituting theft," including theft by false pretense. (Bell v. Feibush (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 1041, 1047-1048 [affirming default judgment for lender in action against borrower for inducing him to loan it money on the false pretense that he owned a trademark and needed the money to settle a lawsuit; borrower's representations were false, and he never repaid the money]; see also Switzer v. Wood (2019) 35 Cal.App.5th 116, 126-128 [holding that member of limited liability company was entitled treble damages under section 496 where managing member deceitfully converted and withheld for himself money and property belonging to cross-complainant and kept the profits for himself].) Defendant's reliance on Siry Investment, L.P. v. Farkhondehpour (2020) 45 Cal.App.5th 1098, review granted, No. S262081 (July 8, 2020), is misplaced, since the California Supreme Court has granted review in that case and it therefore has "no binding or precedential effect, and may be cited for potentially persuasive value only." (Cal. R. Ct. 8.1115(e)(1).) Further, defendant improperly fails to disclose that grant of review, which occurred before the demurrer was filed. (Id. ["Any citation to the Court of Appeal opinion must also note the grant of review and any subsequent action by the Supreme Court."].)

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike By Defendant Latisha Taylor
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LATISHA TAYLOR'S Motion To Strike:

Defendant's motion to strike the prayer for attorneys' fees and treble damages from the first amended complaint is denied, as the accompanying ruling determines that plaintiff has properly pled a cause of action for violation of Penal Code section 496, which expressly authorizes such relief. The motion to strike paragraphs 29-33 of the FAC as referring to settlement communications protected by Evidence Code section 1152 is denied. The referenced paragraphs allege that defendant engaged in a continuing fraudulent scheme.

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
